jobs.title: Truck Driver
jobs.risk_label: Evolving — some tasks automated, core role persists
at_risk_tasks: Some warehouses use automated loading systems
Highway platooning (AI-assisted convoys) is being tested
resilient_tasks: Handling exceptions, accidents, and road hazards
jobs.timeline: Autonomous long-haul on limited highways is closer than urban delivery. Most analysts expect 10–20 years before widespread displacement.

jobs.title: Teacher
jobs.risk_label: Low risk — human relationship is the job
at_risk_tasks: Grading multiple-choice tests and routine assignments
resilient_tasks: Recognizing when a child is struggling emotionally
Mentoring and encouraging students who lack confidence
Adapting lessons in real time to a room of individuals
jobs.timeline: AI is an assistive tool in education, not a replacement. The human relationship between teacher and student is consistently shown to be the primary driver of student outcomes.

jobs.title: Nurse
jobs.risk_label: Low risk — care, judgment, and touch can't be automated
at_risk_tasks: Routine documentation and charting (AI scribes are emerging)
resilient_tasks: Emotional support for patients and families
Coordinating with doctors and families during crises
jobs.timeline: AI helps nurses work more efficiently but cannot replace hands-on care, empathy, or the clinical judgment developed through years of experience.
jobs.source: American Nurses Association, McKinsey Health Institute

jobs.title: Graphic Designer
jobs.risk_label: High risk — AI image tools directly compete with entry-level work
at_risk_tasks: Simple logo variations and icon sets
Basic social media graphics and templates
resilient_tasks: Client communication and translating business needs into design
jobs.timeline: Entry-level graphic design jobs are already declining (21% drop in postings since 2022). Senior and strategic design roles are more protected.

jobs.title: Software Engineer
jobs.risk_label: Evolving — AI writes code, but engineers direct it
at_risk_tasks: Generating unit tests for known patterns
Simple bug fixes for well-understood issues
resilient_tasks: Understanding business requirements and translating them to technical specs
Code review and maintaining quality standards
resilient_skills: ML engineering and AI product development
jobs.timeline: AI coding tools are raising productivity but also raising expectations. Junior roles are most at risk; senior engineers who direct AI tools are in demand.
jobs.source: GitHub, Stack Overflow Developer Survey, Goldman Sachs

jobs.title: Radiologist
jobs.risk_label: Evolving — AI assists interpretation, specialists still required
at_risk_tasks: Screening reads for common, well-defined conditions (e.g. chest X-ray pneumonia)
Routine report generation for normal studies
resilient_tasks: Communicating findings to patients and clinical teams
jobs.timeline: AI is a second reader, not a replacement. Radiologists who adopt AI are more productive; those who don't may be at risk in high-volume routine work.
jobs.source: American College of Radiology, Nature Medicine

jobs.title: Accountant
jobs.risk_label: High risk — routine bookkeeping is nearly fully automatable
at_risk_tasks: Routine tax preparation for standard returns
resilient_tasks: Tax strategy and planning for complex situations
Managing client relationships and understanding business context
jobs.timeline: Routine bookkeeping roles have been declining for years as software handles more. The CPA doing complex advisory work is safer than the data-entry accountant.

jobs.title: Lawyer
jobs.risk_label: Evolving — document work is automating, courtroom and counsel are not
at_risk_tasks: Legal document review and due diligence
resilient_tasks: Client counseling in complex or sensitive situations
Creative legal strategy in novel situations
jobs.timeline: Junior associate work in document review is most at risk. Partner-level strategic counsel and litigation are well-protected.
jobs.source: American Bar Association, Thomson Reuters Institute

jobs.title: Customer Service Representative
jobs.risk_label: High risk — chatbots and voice AI directly replace this work
at_risk_tasks: Processing routine returns, cancellations, and account changes
Routing calls to the right department
resilient_tasks: Handling emotionally difficult or complex situations
Cases requiring judgment and exceptions to policy
jobs.timeline: AI chatbots already handle 60–70% of routine inquiries at many large companies. Tier-1 support roles are declining rapidly; specialist and escalation roles persist.

jobs.title: Data Entry Clerk
jobs.risk_label: Very high risk — this is exactly what AI was built to do
at_risk_tasks: Entering structured data from forms or documents
Transcription of audio or handwritten content
resilient_tasks: Handling unusual exceptions and ambiguous data
Working with stakeholders to understand what data means
jobs.timeline: Automation of data entry has been underway for over a decade. AI has accelerated this significantly. Retraining toward data analysis is strongly recommended.
jobs.source: BLS, World Economic Forum Future of Jobs

jobs.title: Writer
jobs.risk_label: Evolving — AI competes for commodity content, not quality journalism
at_risk_tasks: Generic SEO articles and product descriptions
resilient_tasks: Writing that requires source relationships and trust
Cultural criticism with a distinct voice
jobs.timeline: Commodity content is already largely AI-generated. Quality, voice-driven, and investigative writing is less affected but markets for it are also shrinking.

jobs.title: Plumber
jobs.risk_label: Low risk — physical, variable, and site-specific
at_risk_tasks: Scheduling and dispatching (AI can optimize routing)
Generating invoices and estimates from standard jobs
resilient_tasks: Diagnosing problems in unique, non-standard installations
Physical installation and repair in confined or complex spaces
Working in homes with unpredictable layouts and conditions
jobs.timeline: Physical trades requiring dexterity in unpredictable environments are among the hardest jobs to automate. Demand for skilled tradespeople is growing as the workforce ages.

jobs.title: Electrician
jobs.risk_label: Low risk — hands-on, licensed, and growing in demand
at_risk_tasks: Estimating and quoting standard jobs (software helps)
resilient_tasks: Diagnosing electrical faults in existing buildings
Reading plans and adapting to real-world conditions
jobs.timeline: Electricians are among the most in-demand tradespeople due to EV adoption, solar installation, and aging electrical infrastructure. Automation is not a near-term threat.

jobs.title: Social Worker
jobs.risk_label: Low risk — human judgment and trust are the core of this work
at_risk_tasks: Paperwork and case documentation (AI scribes help)
resilient_tasks: Building trust with vulnerable individuals and families
Assessing risk in complex and fluid situations
Advocating for clients in bureaucratic systems
jobs.timeline: Social work requires human empathy, cultural understanding, and the ability to build trust across deeply challenging circumstances. It cannot be automated.

jobs.title: Financial Analyst
jobs.risk_label: Evolving — data analysis is automating, judgment and communication persist
at_risk_tasks: Pulling and formatting data from multiple sources
Building standard financial models from templates
resilient_tasks: Advising on investment decisions with incomplete information
Spotting unusual patterns that require investigation
jobs.timeline: AI is very good at financial data processing but poor at the judgment, narrative, and relationship-building that senior analysts do.

jobs.title: Paralegal
jobs.risk_label: High risk — document review and research are core AI strengths
resilient_tasks: Complex procedural coordination across multiple courts
Specialized paralegal work in niche practice areas
resilient_skills: Paralegal certification in specialized areas (IP, immigration, real estate)
jobs.timeline: Thomson Reuters found AI can do much of paralegal document work in a fraction of the time. Firms are already reducing junior associate and paralegal head count.
jobs.source: Thomson Reuters Institute, American Bar Association

jobs.title: Pharmacist
jobs.risk_label: Evolving — dispensing is automating, clinical roles are growing
at_risk_tasks: Basic drug interaction checking (now largely automated)
resilient_tasks: Clinical pharmacy in hospital and specialty settings
Complex patient cases with multiple drugs and conditions
jobs.timeline: Automated dispensing is already widespread. Retail pharmacist roles are declining while clinical pharmacist demand grows.

jobs.title: HR Manager
jobs.risk_label: Evolving — administrative HR is automating, strategic HR is not
at_risk_tasks: Resume screening and initial candidate filtering
Answering routine policy questions (AI chatbots handle these)
jobs.timeline: AI hiring tools are already screening resumes, but they've also introduced documented bias. Human oversight of AI in hiring is becoming legally and ethically essential.

jobs.title: Architect
jobs.risk_label: Evolving — design generation is augmented, not replaced
at_risk_tasks: Generating design variations and massing studies
Producing standard details and specifications from templates
resilient_tasks: Understanding client needs and translating them to spatial solutions
Creative problem-solving in complex or constrained sites
jobs.timeline: AI generates design options much faster, but architecture requires human judgment, client relationships, and licensed sign-off. Firms using AI are more competitive.

jobs.title: Physical Therapist
jobs.risk_label: Low risk — hands-on care and therapeutic relationship are the product
at_risk_tasks: Documentation and progress notes (AI scribes help)
resilient_tasks: Assessing movement and pain in the full context of a patient's life
Motivating and coaching patients through difficult recovery
Adapting treatment in real time to patient response
jobs.timeline: Physical rehabilitation requires physical presence, touch, and a therapeutic relationship. Demand for PTs is growing due to aging population and sports medicine.

jobs.title: Chef
jobs.risk_label: Low risk — creativity, craft, and sensory judgment are hard to automate
at_risk_tasks: High-volume fast food preparation (fast food automation is advancing)
jobs.timeline: Automation is entering fast food, but culinary craft in sit-down restaurants depends on creativity and sensory judgment that remains distinctly human.

jobs.title: Warehouse Worker
jobs.risk_label: High risk — Amazon and others are rapidly automating warehouse tasks
at_risk_tasks: Picking items from shelves in organized warehouses
Sorting and routing packages on conveyor systems
resilient_tasks: Handling irregular, fragile, or non-standard items
Maintenance and oversight of automated systems
jobs.timeline: Amazon has deployed over 750,000 robots. The WEF estimates 85 million warehouse and manufacturing jobs are at risk globally by 2025. Retraining is urgent.

jobs.title: Journalist
jobs.risk_label: Evolving — AI writes commodity news, human journalism is irreplaceable
at_risk_tasks: Writing earnings reports and sports scores from structured data
Generating routine local news briefs from press releases
Social media posting and SEO headline variations
resilient_tasks: Synthesizing complex topics for general audiences
resilient_skills: Specialized beat expertise (courts, science, conflict zones)
jobs.timeline: Local news has already been devastated, partly by AI content farms. National investigative journalism at quality outlets is under less direct AI threat but under severe economic pressure.
jobs.source: Reuters Institute, Nieman Lab, Columbia Journalism Review

jobs.title: Real Estate Agent
jobs.risk_label: Evolving — search is automated, relationships and negotiation are not
at_risk_tasks: Property matching and search (now largely algorithmic)
Generating listing descriptions and marketing copy
resilient_tasks: Negotiating deals with complex human dynamics
Building long-term client trust in the largest transaction of their lives
Reading local market nuance that isn't in the data
Handling the emotional complexity of buying and selling homes
jobs.timeline: Platforms like Zillow have reduced the informational advantage agents once held. Agents who add clear value beyond search are better positioned.

jobs.title: Mental Health Therapist
jobs.risk_label: Low risk — therapeutic relationship cannot be replicated
at_risk_tasks: Session notes and clinical documentation (AI scribes help)
Matching patients to therapists based on fit
resilient_tasks: Building the therapeutic alliance — the single strongest predictor of outcomes
Navigating complex trauma and crisis situations
Adapting evidence-based approaches to the individual
Making safety assessments in suicidal ideation cases
jobs.timeline: AI mental health chatbots (Character.AI, Woebot, etc.) are being scrutinized for causing harm. Licensed therapists are the gold standard and demand exceeds supply in most markets.
jobs.source: APA, NASW, National Alliance on Mental Illness

high.label: High Risk
high.message: AI can already do significant portions of this work. This doesn't mean your job disappears tomorrow — but it's worth thinking about which skills to develop.

medium.label: Evolving
medium.message: Parts of this role are automating, but the core work requiring judgment, relationships, or physical presence is holding up. Focus on what AI can't do.

low.label: Low Risk
low.message: This role is among the more resilient to AI automation. Human presence, physical dexterity, care, or irreplaceable judgment are at the center of the work.
